Comment.MultiLanguageText: Module F teach-point slots (1..16) -- CARTESIAN. Each slot reuses the Siemens-canonical LKinCtrl_typePoint UDT: name (WString), position[0..5] LReal (Cartesian X/Y/Z/A/B/C in WCS; SCARA populates [0..3] only), coordSystem USInt (default 0=WCS), linkConstellation DWord, turnJoint[0..5] DInt. FB_TeachCtrl writes here on operator's bo_Capture edge from GDB_AxisCtrl.LKinCtrl.output.actualposition. Replay (bo_StartReplay) walks these via Cartesian linear moves.

V1.1 (2026-05-23, joint extension per Phase 2 §7.1 spec "TCP/关节"): Per-slot joint angles J1/J2/J3/J4 captured at the same scan as aPoints[i]. 2D array (S7-1500T does not allow nested Array-of-Array in interface declarations -- same convention LKinCtrl_FrameTransTool's rotation matrices use). Access: aJointAngles[slot, joint] e.g. aJointAngles[3, 1] = slot 3's J1. FB_TeachCtrl writes from "ScaraArm3D".AxesData.A[1..4].Position on the same bo_Capture rising edge. Stored for archival + future joint-space PTP replay (currently replay is Cartesian-linear only). J1/J2/J4 in degrees, J3 in mm (prismatic Z per the SCARA TO config).

Per-slot "this slot has been captured" flag. FB_TeachCtrl sets TRUE on bo_Capture rising edge for slot i16_SlotIdx (one set covers BOTH aPoints[idx] and aJointAngles[idx], V1.1); clears on bo_Clear or bo_ClearAll. Replay (bo_StartReplay) skips slots where this is FALSE. Default FALSE (no slots captured at boot).

FB-computed status: COUNT of TRUE bits in abCaptured[1..16]. HMI displays as "Captured slots: N / 16". Also used by FB_TeachCtrl Replay_FSM as the bound for the slot iteration (replay only runs slots where abCaptured = TRUE).

AttributeList.Text: SCARA Phase 2 Module F teach-point storage (V1.1, 2026-05-23). 16 slots, each carrying BOTH Cartesian TCP (aPoints[i] = LKinCtrl_typePoint, Siemens-canonical) AND joint angles (aJointAngles[i] = Array[1..4] of LReal for J1-J4). V1.1 added joint capture per Phase 2 spec §7.1 ("捕获脉冲 → 当前 TCP/关节 写点表"). Operator captures both in one bo_Capture pulse via FB_TeachCtrl. Replay (bo_StartReplay) currently walks aPoints via Cartesian linear moves; joint-space PTP replay using aJointAngles is reserved for a future extension.
